market_summary.narrative: U.S. equities rallied for a second consecutive week as the S&P 500 closed at 7,165.08 on Friday, April 24, supported by an earnings season that broadly exceeded depressed expectations. The catalyst of the week was Intel's blowout Q1 result — its best earnings day since 1987 — which unleashed a wave of semiconductor buying that carried ARM, AMD, Marvell, and the broader AI infrastructure complex to multi-week highs. GE Vernova hit an all-time high mid-week after raising full-year power revenue guidance, underscoring persistent AI data-center electricity demand. UnitedHealth's earnings beat arrested a multi-week slide in managed care, while Baker Hughes surged on record IET orders, reinforcing the energy-infrastructure capex cycle. On the downside, Northrop Grumman's ongoing B-21 program charge and production-volume risks drove the stock to its worst week in years, and Thermo Fisher sold off sharply on anemic 1% organic growth. ServiceNow's strong subscription beat was overshadowed by elevated SaaS valuation anxiety, sending it lower. Macro conditions remain broadly supportive: the 10-year Treasury yield eased to 4.31%, VIX fell to 18.71 — well below the March spike — and oil stabilized above $94/bbl (WTI) amid Iran-related geopolitical risk. The FOMC meets April 29; consensus expects a hold. Mega-cap tech (META, GOOG, MSFT, AMZN) reports next week, and the market will scrutinize AI capex commentary closely.

russell_2000.ytd_note: Lagging large-cap; credit-sensitive names weighed on small-caps

market_summary.regime_description: Equities at or near all-time highs driven by AI infrastructure capex; earnings beats concentrated in technology and energy infrastructure. Macro headwinds include elevated oil (Iran tensions), a Fed on hold with limited near-term cut visibility, and widening credit cost normalization in consumer financials. VIX normalization and a steeper risk-on tilt in semiconductors (+40% ARM, +25% AMD on the week) contrast with defensive names under pressure (NOC -14%, DHR -9%, TMO -11%). The breadth of earnings season beats (approx. 78% beat rate ex-financials) suggests the AI capex cycle remains intact through mid-2026, though services-software valuation compression (NOW, IBM) signals investor rotation toward proven cash-flow generators.

ten_year_yield.change_note: Down ~10 bps on the week; Fed hold expected April 29

vix.change_note: Sharply lower than March spike (~30); market de-risking in progress

wti_crude.change_note: Elevated on sustained Iran-supply-disruption risk; WTI ~$95, Brent ~$105

value_picks.name: Global Payments Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Global Payments closed its transformative Worldpay acquisition and Issuer Solutions divestiture in January 2026, becoming a pure-play commerce solutions provider. The resulting accounting noise — revenue restated, new shares issued, integration costs front-loaded — has depressed GAAP reported earnings and confused consensus models. The stock trades at just ~4.8x fiscal 2026E adjusted EPS guidance of $13.80–$14.00, a multi-decade trough multiple for a company with $2B+ in annual free cash flow. Integration concerns over Worldpay (acquired at a $24.25B EV) weigh on sentiment, though Worldpay is a structurally sound global payment platform with best-in-class merchant acquiring economics. The market is pricing in worst-case integration failure, while the pro forma business has guided for ~5% adjusted revenue growth and meaningful margin expansion.
value_picks.bull_case: GPN is reshaping itself into a focused, high-margin commerce solutions company after shedding its lower-growth issuer business for $13.5B and adding Worldpay's global reach. At normalized EV/EBITDA of 9–10x on a combined EBITDA expected to exceed $5B in 2027, the stock could re-rate toward analyst consensus targets of $90+, representing 35%+ upside. The Worldpay brand is one of the most recognized in global merchant services, and cross-selling synergies across GPN's software vertical reach and Worldpay's scale are substantial. As integration de-risks over a 12–18 month horizon, multiple expansion from the current near-trough valuation appears highly probable. Free cash flow of $2B+ gives management ample capacity to reduce leverage and return capital while the strategic story matures.

value_picks.name: IQVIA Holdings Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: IQVIA is the largest contract research organization (CRO) and healthcare data analytics company in the world, yet its stock has fallen 34% from its 52-week high as investors worried about pharma R&D spending caution, emerging biotech funding pressures, and slower-than-expected clinical trial bookings following post-COVID normalization. These headwinds are real but temporary and well-known — the stock has already priced them in at just 15x EV/EBITDA, well below its historical average of 20–25x. Revenue grew 5.9% in FY2025 to $16.3B, and the commercial solutions segment is guided to 7–9% growth as drug launches recover. The company's proprietary IQVIA OCE platform and real-world evidence datasets represent a unique moat that cannot be easily replicated — embedded switching costs are extremely high.
value_picks.bull_case: IQVIA sits at the intersection of two secular growth trends: pharmaceutical R&D outsourcing (now >60% of industry spend) and the monetization of real-world healthcare data. With 90% of analyst ratings bullish and an average target of $225.80 — implying 39% upside — the Street broadly views current weakness as a buying opportunity. IQVIA's new AI collaboration with NVIDIA and its Orchestrated Clinical Trials platform are unlocking faster trial design and execution, a potential margin catalyst over the 1–3 year horizon. As biotech funding stabilizes and pharma project cancellation rates revert toward normal, the backlog (currently >$30B) should translate into accelerating revenue recognition. A re-rating to 20x EV/EBITDA on growing EBITDA of $3.2B+ would imply a stock price above $230.

value_picks.name: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: GE HealthCare was spun off from GE in January 2023 and trades at a significant discount to pure-play medtech peers despite commanding #1 or #2 market share in MRI, CT, ultrasound, and molecular imaging. The stock has drifted from $90 to near its 52-week low of $67 as investors remain skeptical of the post-spinoff leverage (D/E ~1.0x) and uncertain about China hospital spending trends and U.S. hospital capex cycles. However, FY2025 revenue grew 4.8% to $20.6B with expanding operating margins, and FCF generation of $1.5B is improving annually as the company rightsizes its cost structure. At 12.9x EV/EBITDA — well below Siemens Healthineers and Philips — the market is not crediting GEHC's durable brand moat, backlog stability, or long-tailed service contract revenues.
value_picks.bull_case: GEHC has a uniquely durable franchise: hospitals replace their imaging fleets every 10–15 years and rely on GE HealthCare for service contracts, upgrades, and AI-enabled workflow software. The AI in Imaging segment is an underappreciated incremental revenue stream — GEHC has over 100 FDA-cleared AI-powered solutions embedded in its equipment. As the company continues to execute on its cost transformation program and improves FCF conversion, the multiple gap vs. peers should narrow. Eight of thirteen covering analysts rate the stock Buy with an average target of $88.77 — 29% above current levels — and GE's legacy brand ownership across global hospital systems provides a defensive competitive position unlikely to erode over a 1–3 year horizon.

value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Cognizant has been serially overlooked because investors fear that generative AI will commoditize traditional IT outsourcing and erode its $21B revenue base. This concern has driven the stock down 37% from its 52-week high to barely above its 52-week low of $54.26, creating a rare valuation anomaly: CTSH trades at just 10.8x 2026E EPS and ~7.6x EV/EBITDA, historically cheap for a business generating $2.5B+ in annual free cash flow with essentially zero net debt. In reality, the company's Q4 2025 bookings hit a record — 12 deals above $100M contract value and a 1.3x book-to-bill ratio — and it crossed $20B in annual revenue for the first time. Management is actively repositioning toward AI-augmented services via its AI Factory platform co-built with NVIDIA and Dell infrastructure.
value_picks.bull_case: Cognizant's AI Factory launch in March 2026 positions the company to capture the fast-growing agentic AI and BPO market, where revenue is priced per outcome rather than per headcount — a more defensible and margin-accretive model. With FY2026 revenue guided to $22.1B–$22.7B (5–8% growth) and EPS guided to $5.56–$5.70, a return to even 14x forward earnings — below the 15–18x historical range — implies a stock price of $78–$82, matching consensus targets. The Belcan engineering acquisition adds defense-sector exposure (a $150B addressable ceiling), diversifying the revenue base beyond financial services and healthcare. CTSH's ~$4B share repurchase capacity (net cash balance sheet) further supports per-share value compounding at current depressed price levels.

value_picks.name: The Mosaic Company
value_picks.why_undervalued: Mosaic is the world's largest producer of finished potash and phosphate fertilizers — essential inputs with no viable substitutes for global food production. The stock has fallen 37% from its 52-week high and sits just above a multi-year low of $23.06, pricing in sustained weakness in potash and phosphate spot prices that has materialized since the post-COVID commodity supercycle peak. However, FY2025 revenue grew 8.4% to $12.1B, and structural demand from the global food supply imperative remains intact: the UN projects global population to exceed 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring a 50% increase in agricultural output. At just 4.1x EV/EBITDA and a ~10% FCF yield, Mosaic offers deep margin-of-safety valuation with nearly zero net leverage (D/E ~0.20).
value_picks.bull_case: Mosaic's moat is geological — potash and phosphate reserves are finite, concentrated in a handful of countries, and extremely capital-intensive to develop, creating a durable oligopoly with Nutrien and OCP. Any sustained improvement in fertilizer pricing (driven by natural gas feedstock costs in Europe, geopolitical supply disruption, or agricultural commodity price recovery) would produce outsized earnings leverage given the fixed-cost-heavy production base. The company has been buying back shares aggressively at current depressed prices and recently boosted its dividend. A normalization of potash prices toward the $350–$400/tonne mid-cycle band from today's ~$280–$300 would add approximately $3–4 per share in annual EPS, easily justifying a stock price 40–50% above current levels within a 1–3 year horizon.

momentum_picks.name: Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd
momentum_picks.catalyst: Credo is the purest AI data-center connectivity play outside the avoid list, supplying active electrical cable (AEC) interconnects that replace expensive optical transceivers inside hyperscaler GPU clusters. Q3 fiscal 2026 (ended Jan 31, 2026) delivered revenue of $407M, up 201.5% year-over-year and 51.9% sequentially, with GAAP gross margins of 68.5% — an exceptionally high level for a hardware company. Management guided Q4 fiscal 2026 revenue of $425–$435M, implying continued sequential growth. MarketBeat reports 84% buy-side analyst bias across 18 analysts tracking the name, and institutional accumulation ran 3:1 buy/sell in Q1 2026. The stock pulled back sharply in March 2026 alongside the broader tech sell-off to the $88–$96 range before rebounding aggressively, making this week's +21% move a catch-up trade underpinned by real demand acceleration. AEC adoption is in early innings; hyperscalers at scale need thousands of these per rack.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: At ~$195, CRDO trades at a high multiple relative to current earnings given the company is still in hyper-growth mode. The key risk is growth deceleration: Q4 guidance implies only ~5% sequential growth, which caused the stock to sell off post-Q3 earnings before the market recalibrated. Customer concentration in a handful of hyperscalers (Microsoft, Amazon) means any CapEx pause or vendor shift could be outsized. On the upside, the company has nearly $814M in cash, no debt, and accelerating free cash flow — it is self-funding. A buy-side analyst median price target (from MarketBeat) of roughly $200+ gives modest near-term upside from current levels; the 1–3 year case rests on AEC becoming the standard interconnect for 800G/1.6T rack deployments, which would grow Credo's addressable market materially. Valuation risk is real if hyperscaler CapEx peaks, but the structural shift to higher-bandwidth connectivity provides a durable tailwind. Sector: Technology / AI Connectivity Semiconductors. Market cap: ~$36B (large-cap).

momentum_picks.name: Kaiser Aluminum Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Kaiser Aluminum delivered a blowout Q1 2026 on April 22, 2026 — the single largest reported catalyst of the week. Net sales surged to $1.11B (up 42% YoY vs. $777M a year prior), EPS of $3.74 crushed the $1.96 consensus by 91%, and Adjusted EBITDA rose to $128.5M with a 31.8% margin on Conversion Revenue, both quarterly records. Volume growth was driven by a surge in aerospace/defense demand as destocking eased and aircraft build rates normalized, plus strong coated packaging demand. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance: Conversion Revenue +10%–15% YoY and Adjusted EBITDA +20%–30% YoY. KeyCorp upgraded its price target to $183 with an Overweight rating and Zacks moved to #1 Strong Buy. The stock is a direct beneficiary of domestic aerospace production recovery — Boeing's production ramp and defense orders provide a multi-year demand floor that is independent of tariff risk.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: KALU trades at roughly 19–20x trailing earnings post-earnings re-rate, which remains modest for an industrial with surging margins. The $2.87B market cap and mid-cap positioning mean institutional ownership is still growing — a longer-term tailwind. Key downside risks: aluminum commodity price volatility (metal is a pass-through in the business model, but execution of hedging matters); a Boeing production stumble or Airbus rate cut would compress aerospace demand; and the consensus rating remains technically a Hold (average target ~$148) with many sell-side analysts not yet updating models for the Q1 beat. For 1–3 year holders, the re-rating case is compelling — Adjusted EBITDA was on a depressed base in 2025 and should compound at double digits annually if aerospace continues its up-cycle. The declared $0.77 quarterly dividend ($3.08 annualized, ~1.7% yield) provides downside support. Sector: Materials / Specialty Aluminum. Market cap: ~$2.9B (mid-cap).

momentum_picks.name: Valmont Industries, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Valmont Industries reported Q1 2026 earnings on April 21, 2026 that meaningfully beat on all metrics: revenue of $1.03B (+6.2% YoY, 3% above consensus), GAAP EPS of $5.51 (+27.5% YoY, 18.1% beat vs. $4.67 consensus), and Adjusted EBITDA of $178.2M (9.1% above estimates). The infrastructure segment — 78% of sales — grew 14.1% YoY driven by a 27.4% surge in North America Utility as grid modernization and electrification spending accelerated. Management raised the lower end of full-year EPS guidance to $21.50–$23.50 (midpoint $22.50). Backlog remained elevated at $1.65B. The stock surged from $412 to nearly $499 this week — one of the strongest single-week moves in the name's recent history — as investors recognized VMI as a direct play on America's grid buildout, data-center power infrastructure, and utility capex supercycle. VMI manufactures the steel transmission structures and poles that support both traditional and AI-driven electricity demand.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: VMI's $8–9B market cap and ~24x P/E reflect a premium for infrastructure secular tailwinds, but the earnings beat quality was broad and margin-driven (operating margin expanded 190 bps to 15.1%). The agriculture segment (~22% of revenue) remains a drag — sales fell 15.1% YoY on soft international markets — and represents the key overhang for FY2026. However, utility infrastructure backlog is near record levels and rising energy demand from AI data centers provides a sustained multiyear demand driver independent of commodity cycles. A potential risk is cost inflation in steel inputs, though VMI's pricing discipline has demonstrated the ability to pass through costs. Analysts project EPS of ~$22–$23 for FY2026, suggesting the stock trades at roughly 22x forward earnings — reasonable for a capital-efficient infrastructure compounder with 13%-per-quarter dividend growth. For 1–3 year holders, grid spending is a decade-long tailwind. Sector: Industrials / Infrastructure Manufacturing. Market cap: ~$9.1B (large-cap).

momentum_picks.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Onto Innovation is a semiconductor process-control equipment maker that sits directly in the AI packaging supply chain. On April 16, 2026, the company announced preliminary Q1 2026 revenue of $292M — above its prior guidance range of $275–$285M and ahead of the $280M consensus — and simultaneously raised Q2 2026 guidance to $320–$330M, an 8% increase versus its previously communicated outlook. The key product catalyst is the Dragonfly G5 inspection platform, recently qualified at a major customer for 2.5D advanced packaging (the technology used in AI GPU stacks like Nvidia's CoWoS). Management expects Dragonfly platform demand to grow more than 50% in 2026 vs. 2025. Multiple analyst firms raised targets: Needham to $320, Stifel to $350, Cantor Fitzgerald to $300, and Evercore to $315. Reports indicate Onto passed TSMC's New Tool Selection Committee, and the company also has a $240M HBM volume purchase agreement covering 2027. With 98% institutional ownership, the buyer base is high-conviction long-only.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: At $307 and a P/E of ~105x trailing earnings, ONTO carries an elevated multiple that requires consistent execution. The FY2026 consensus EPS of ~$6.72 puts the forward P/E around 45x — still premium but more defensible if the revenue ramp to $320–$330M/quarter is maintained. Downside risk includes customer concentration (HBM and CoWoS volumes tied to 2–3 chip makers) and a potential WFE spending pause if AI CapEx moderates. However, the Dragonfly G5 qualification at TSMC and SK Hynix, combined with the secured $240M volume agreement through 2027, provides substantial revenue visibility — an unusual quality in the equipment sector. For 1–3 year holders, AI packaging inspection is one of the fastest-growing niches in all of semiconductor capital equipment as chiplet architectures proliferate. The stock's 154% gain over the past year (per Zacks) reflects a genuine re-rating, not momentum alone. Sector: Technology / Semiconductor Equipment. Market cap: ~$8.5B (large-cap).

momentum_picks.name: Avnet, Inc.
momentum_picks.revenue_growth: +11.6% YoY (Q2 FY2026, reported Jan 28, 2026); Asia segment +17% YoY
momentum_picks.catalyst: Avnet is the world's second-largest electronic components distributor and is riding the broadening electronics demand recovery that is spreading from pure AI compute into aerospace, defense, industrial automation, and general electronics restocking. Q2 FY2026 (reported January 28) delivered $6.3B in revenue (+12% YoY), EPS of $1.05 vs. $0.95 consensus, and record Asia revenues exceeding 50% of total sales for the first time. Management guided Q3 FY2026 at $6.2–$6.5B with EPS of $1.20–$1.30 — implying further sequential improvement in the higher-margin Americas and EMEA regions. The stock broke out from a decade-long sideways trading range to new all-time highs in early 2026 with a 60% YTD gain (per Zacks). Lead times are trending higher across most product categories — a reliable early-cycle indicator that pre-orders are accelerating. The demand-creation business (field application engineers driving design wins) grew 7% sequentially, suggesting revenue will be supported by converted design wins in the next 4–6 quarters.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: AVT's ~$6.3B market cap and low-teen P/E make it one of the most attractively valued names on this list. Distribution businesses typically trade at modest multiples given thin gross margins (~10.5%), but the operating leverage is significant as the cycle turns: AVT is guiding EPS growth from $1.05 (Q2) to $1.20–$1.30 (Q3) — 14–24% sequential growth with minimal incremental costs. The main risk is a sharp reversal in AI CapEx or a demand air pocket that causes customers to cancel backlog orders, as happened in 2023–2024. AVT went through a difficult destocking period in 2023–24 during which revenue fell sharply; any macro re-softening would compress margins quickly. Analyst consensus is a Hold with a ~$59–$60 median target — below the current $78 price — meaning AVT has already broken above institutional consensus, which is either a red flag or confirmation of a re-rating that sell-side models haven't absorbed. For 1–3 year investors, the electronics upcycle typically lasts multiple years once confirmed, and Asia's 50%+ share of revenues positions AVT for continued growth as Asian data-center and manufacturing expansion accelerates. Sector: Technology / Electronic Components Distribution. Market cap: ~$6.3B (large-cap).

key_trends.title: AI Infrastructure Buildout Drives Semiconductor Super-Cycle
key_trends.description: The week's dominant theme was a broad-based semiconductor re-rating triggered by Intel's historic Q1 earnings beat and ARM's Investor Day revelation of its own AGI CPU roadmap targeting $25B in revenue by FY2031. ARM surged 41%, AMD 25%, Marvell 18%, ALAB 22%, and MU 9% as investors marked up the entire AI silicon supply chain. Multiple sell-side analysts raised ARM price targets to $240 (Citi, Guggenheim, Barclays, Evercore ISI). GE Vernova's all-time high and BKR's record IET orders ($4.9B) reinforced the downstream power/energy-infrastructure thesis. The AMD-ARM day-on-Friday alone accounted for a material portion of the Nasdaq's weekly outperformance vs. the S&P 500.

key_trends.title: Managed Care Recovers; Health-Tech Diverges on Organic Growth Quality
key_trends.description: UnitedHealth's Q1 beat (adj. EPS $7.23 vs. $6.57 est.) and raised FY2026 guidance (>$18.25 adj. EPS) reversed several weeks of managed-care underperformance, with UNH gaining 9.3% and ELV +6.7% in sympathy. In contrast, Thermo Fisher (-10.8%) and Danaher (-9.0%) both reported beats but guided to continued sub-2% organic core growth, highlighting investor impatience with life-sciences recovery timing. Abbott's slight Q1 beat failed to stem its slide (-5.9%) amid integration costs from the Exact Sciences acquisition.

key_trends.title: Energy Commodity Bifurcation: Oil-Services Surge, Miners Punished
key_trends.description: Oil-field services (BKR +15%) and E&P names (DVN +8%, FANG +8%, OXY +6%, VLO +5.5%) rallied on elevated crude prices driven by Middle East tensions. In contrast, Freeport-McMoRan (-13%) collapsed despite a Q1 earnings beat as management cut 2026 copper and gold guidance by ~300 million pounds and 150,000 ounces respectively, citing continued Grasberg mine-ramp delays following the September 2025 mud-rush incident. Gold miners (AEM -9%, NEM +4%) diverged — AEM sold off on volume concerns while NEM benefited from spot gold strength.

key_trends.title: SaaS / Cloud Valuation Reset: Revenue Quality Under the Microscope
key_trends.description: A divergence emerged within enterprise software: names with proven AI-native monetization (CRWD +5.7%, PANW +6.4%, SNPS +11.4%, CDNS +7%) re-rated higher, while those seen as transitional or with mixed AI traction sold off hard. ServiceNow (-6.7%) delivered an in-line Q1 with a raised guide but investors punished the stock on valuation, post-acquisition integration noise, and a deceleration in new-logo ARR. IBM (-8.5%) beat estimates but held full-year guidance flat rather than raising it, interpreted as a signal that AI software revenue ramps are slower than hoped. ACN (-9.8%) hit a new 52-week low as IT consulting spend remains under pressure from corporate AI build-vs-buy decisions.

key_trends.title: Consumer Finance Under Pressure: Elevated Credit Costs and NIM Compression
key_trends.description: Capital One's Q1 miss on revenue ($15.2B vs. $15.4B est.) and elevated charge-offs ($3.8B, net charge-off rate 3.45%) sent the stock down 7.3% despite Discover integration progress. Airlines (UAL -8.6%) faced a twin headwind of surging jet-fuel costs (targeting $4.30/gallon in Q2) and widened full-year guidance ($7-$11 EPS vs. prior $12-$14), as the Iran conflict disrupted both supply costs and Middle East route economics. Consumer-facing fintech (AFRM -1.2%) showed relative resilience but faces its own catalyst at the May 7 earnings report.

risks.title: Iran Conflict Escalation / Oil-Supply Shock
risks.description: WTI crude at ~$95/bbl and Brent at ~$105/bbl reflect a significant risk premium tied to the active U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran. A further escalation — particularly involving Strait of Hormuz disruption or Iranian retaliation against Gulf energy infrastructure — could push crude above $120/bbl, triggering stagflationary pressures and a rapid re-pricing of equities. United Airlines' revised guidance already prices in $4.30/gallon jet fuel for Q2, and airlines have removed ~5 points of capacity. The macro risk is asymmetric: a de-escalation could be a meaningful tailwind, while escalation would hit both consumer confidence and corporate margins simultaneously.
risks.impact: High — affects transportation, consumer discretionary, industrials, and could trigger a Fed policy recalibration if core inflation re-accelerates

risks.title: AI Capex Durability / Hyperscaler Earnings Disappointment
risks.description: The single biggest binary risk for the week ahead is the April 29 reporting day for META, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon. Markets have priced in sustained AI capex (data center, networking, silicon) through at least 2027-2028. Any guidance reduction in AI infrastructure spending — particularly from Microsoft Azure or Google Cloud — could trigger a sharp reversal in the semiconductor supply chain (ARM, AMD, MRVL, ALAB, NVDA) that gained 20-40% over the past week. The risk is not necessarily weak results but a guidance tone that implies AI ROI scrutiny is beginning to moderate investment.
risks.impact: Very High — a 5-10% correction in mega-cap tech would erase the week's index gains and reverse semiconductor momentum

risks.title: Life Sciences / Biopharma Organic Growth Stall
risks.description: Both Thermo Fisher (1% core organic growth, -10.8% on the week) and Danaher (0.5% non-GAAP core growth, -9.0%) reported results that confirm the biopharma instrument and reagent cycle remains sluggish. With CDMOs and large biotech still managing elevated inventory from the COVID-era overbuild and NIH funding uncertainty (potential DOGE-era budget cuts), the recovery timeline keeps getting pushed right. A sustained sub-2% organic growth environment would pressure the multi-billion-dollar capex plans these companies have announced for 2026-2027.
risks.impact: Medium — concentrated in life sciences tools and CRO/CDMO names; could weigh on broader healthcare sector sentiment

risks.title: Defense Procurement Risk: B-21 and Program Overruns
risks.description: Northrop Grumman's 13.5% weekly decline — driven by a $157M Q1 EAC (Estimate at Completion) charge on the B-21 Raider program and no resolution in sight on block-buy pricing — signals that next-generation weapons program overruns are becoming a systemic issue. With the Pentagon under budget pressure and the B-21 still in early low-rate production, investors are right to question whether Northrop can recover margins before 2027-2028. The risk extends to peers with fixed-price development programs.
risks.impact: Medium — primarily NOC-specific near-term, but creates contagion risk for other prime contractors on complex fixed-price programs (LMT, RTX)

risks.title: Copper Production Disruption / Grasberg Mine Risk
risks.description: FCX's -13.1% weekly decline on its Grasberg guidance cut highlights a structural supply risk in copper — a metal critical to electrification, AI data center power infrastructure, and EVs. The Grasberg mine (world's largest copper/gold mine) has now missed two consecutive guidance cycles following the September 2025 mud-rush incident. If copper supply recovery is further delayed while demand from AI infrastructure buildout accelerates, the commodity price upside could be significant — but FCX's ability to capture it depends on operations, not pricing. Longer-term bulls note that copper at current prices supports aggressive volume recovery incentives.
risks.impact: Medium — commodity supply chain risk for electrification themes and mining sector positioning

watchlist_updates.headline: ARM Investor Day reveals AGI CPU roadmap targeting $25B revenue by FY2031; multi-analyst target raises to $240
watchlist_updates.detail: ARM held its Investor Day, introducing its first in-house Arm AGI CPU, targeting $15B in AI-related revenue by FY2031 and total company revenue approaching $25B. Citi, Guggenheim, Evercore ISI, Mizuho, Barclays, Needham, and Susquehanna all raised price targets, with the bull case reaching $240/share. Additional tailwind came Friday from the AMD/INTC AI-CPU narrative lift. Sources: https://stockstotrade.com/news/arm-holdings-plc-arm-news-2026_04_24-2/
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: ARM's 74% 30-day move is one of the most aggressive re-ratings of any large-cap this cycle, driven by a three-stage thesis: (1) royalty upside from premium AI chip architectures (v9 CPUs now >25% of royalty mix), (2) the AGI CPU announcement as a credible entry into chip manufacturing, and (3) a scarcity premium as the only pure-play CPU IP company. Durability risk is high at current valuation — the $25B revenue target is a FY2031 aspiration requiring successful hyperscaler customer wins for a product that does not yet exist. Near-term, the stock is trading on optionality and investor conviction in the AGI transition; any Q4 FY2026 earnings guidance that disappoints on near-term royalty growth could trigger a sharp reversal.

watchlist_updates.headline: AMD surges 25% on the week on AI-CPU demand acceleration narrative and sector re-rating from INTC beat, ARM Investor Day
watchlist_updates.detail: AMD did not report this week but benefited directly from Intel's Q1 beat and ARM's AGI CPU Investor Day, which both amplified the narrative that AI-era CPU demand is accelerating. On Friday, AMD surged 12% alone as investors modeled higher AI data center CPU TAM. The MI350/MI400 GPU pipeline and EPYC server CPU share gains remain the core investment thesis. Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/amd-arm-stocks-rally-shifting-120006035.html
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: AMD's 69% 30-day return places it among the top-performing large-cap stocks in the market this quarter. The move reflects two overlapping drivers: (1) sector re-rating as the semiconductor AI cycle accelerates beyond GPUs into CPUs and custom silicon, and (2) AMD-specific share gain in both EPYC server CPUs (estimated 30%+ datacenter market share) and MI-series AI accelerators. The $347 price embeds significant FY2027 earnings acceleration; the key durability question is whether AMD's Q1 results on May 6 confirm 40%+ AI segment revenue growth. A miss on MI350 ramp or guidance commentary suggesting competitive pressure from NVIDIA GB200 could re-set the rally.

watchlist_updates.headline: Astera Labs surges 22% on week, 75% in 30 days — pure-play AI connectivity IC beneficiary of hyperscaler capex cycle
watchlist_updates.detail: No standalone earnings this week; ALAB rallied in sympathy with the broader semiconductor AI trade. Astera Labs makes PCIe, CXL, and optical connectivity ICs that are critical to large-scale AI cluster fabric. The stock has been a consistent outperformer since its 2024 IPO as hyperscaler AI clusters scale in complexity and bandwidth requirements.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: ALAB's 74.8% 30-day return reflects its position as one of the highest-beta plays on AI data center interconnect — a market growing faster than GPU compute itself as cluster sizes scale from thousands to hundreds of thousands of GPUs. The company's CXL memory-expansion and PCIe switch products address the memory bandwidth bottleneck that becomes critical at scale. At current valuation (30x+ FY2027 revenue), durability depends entirely on hyperscaler cluster size growth continuing on its current trajectory; any slowdown in AI capex would compress the multiple sharply.

watchlist_updates.headline: Q1 2026 massive beat: revenue $13.6B vs. $12.36B est., adj. EPS $0.29 vs. $0.01 est. — best single-day gain since 1987
watchlist_updates.detail: Intel reported Q1 2026 revenue of $13.6B (vs. $12.36B consensus), adj. EPS of $0.29 (vs. $0.01 est.), and raised full-year guidance. CEO Pat Gelsinger credited an accelerating foundry pipeline and robust data-center CPU demand. The stock surged 20.5% on Friday April 24 alone, its best single session since 1987, on massive short-covering and institutional rotation back into the stock. Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/24/intel-stock-soars-more-than-20percent-as-chipmaker-shows-signs-of-turnaround.html
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: INTC's 87% 30-day move reflects a confluence of short-squeeze dynamics and genuine fundamental inflection. The $13.6B revenue result was >10% above consensus, signaling that the data-center CPU upgrade cycle — driven by AI training workloads requiring host-side compute — arrived faster than feared. Foundry capacity commitments from three hyperscaler names (undisclosed) were cited on the call, lending credibility to the IFS ramp. Durability depends on whether Q2 can hold the revenue run-rate above $13B and whether the foundry pipeline converts into external revenue before 2027 — near-term, the bear case centers on margin dilution from ramping IFS capacity.

watchlist_updates.headline: Marvell +17.6% on week, +78% in 30 days — reports helping Google with two new custom AI chips (CNBC April 20)
watchlist_updates.detail: Reports emerged April 20 that Marvell Technology is collaborating with Alphabet/Google on two new custom AI chips, driving a 50%+ April rally. This follows fiscal 2026 revenue of $8.2B (record, +42% YoY) and CEO commentary that 'growth was driven by robust AI demand' with acceleration expected each quarter in FY2027. Next catalyst: Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 28. Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/20/marvell-stock-google-custom-ai-chips.html
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: MRVL's 78% 30-day return is anchored in a fundamental shift from a commodity networking chip supplier to a custom silicon design partner for hyperscalers. The Google collaboration — reportedly involving two distinct AI accelerator programs — would make Marvell's custom ASIC franchise comparable in revenue scale to Broadcom's by FY2028 if fully ramped. Record $8.2B FY2026 revenue with AI segment growing >80% YoY provides earnings cover for the valuation. The key durability risk is customer concentration — if Google's internal TPU team absorbs one or both programs, Marvell's revenue ramp timeline shifts materially.

watchlist_updates.headline: Baker Hughes Q1 2026 beat: adj. EPS $0.58 vs. $0.50 est.; IET orders record $4.9B, EBITDA margin +140 bps YoY
watchlist_updates.detail: Baker Hughes Q1 2026: adj. EPS $0.58 (vs. $0.50 consensus), adj. EBITDA margins expanded 140 bps YoY. Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) segment orders hit a record $4.9B, driven by LNG equipment and gas-compression demand for AI-data-center power. Raised full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance. Stock surged 15.3% on the week. Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/energy/articles/baker-hughes-company-q1-2026-123000486.html

watchlist_updates.headline: GE Vernova Q1 2026 beat: revenue $9.3B, adj. EBITDA +87% YoY to $896M, FCF $4.8B — raised FY guidance, stock hit ATH
watchlist_updates.detail: GE Vernova Q1 2026: revenue $9.3B (+24% YoY), adj. EBITDA $896M (+87% YoY), FCF $4.8B. Raised 2026 revenue guidance to $44.5-$45.5B. Gas turbine orders hit another record as utility customers prioritize dispatchable power for AI data centers. Stock reached all-time high on April 22. Source: https://www.wsj.com/business/earnings/ge-vernova-lifts-outlook-on-accelerating-power-demand-d81d21e8
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: GEV's 26.4% 30-day gain reflects the market's conviction that AI data center electricity demand has created a multi-year gas turbine super-cycle. The $4.8B quarterly FCF result was transformational — management is now guiding to full-year FCF that would imply $18-20B in 4-year cumulative cash generation, enabling aggressive capital returns and debt paydown. The gas turbine order backlog (reported at 9+ years of production capacity) provides extraordinary revenue visibility. Durability risk is execution (Onshore Wind losses need to continue narrowing) and any policy shift away from gas infrastructure — neither appears imminent.

watchlist_updates.headline: Northrop Grumman Q1 2026 slight beat masks ongoing B-21 EAC charge ($157M); stock -13.6% — worst week in years
watchlist_updates.detail: Northrop Grumman Q1 2026: EPS $6.14 (vs. $6.12 est.), revenue $9.88B. The B-21 Raider program continues to generate cumulative EAC charges ($157M in Q1 alone), reflecting cost overruns on the fixed-price development contract. Management reaffirmed FY2026 guidance but provided no resolution timeline for B-21 cost normalization. The stock declined 13.6% on the week. Source: https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-northrop-grumman-beats-q1-2026-forecasts-stock-dips-93CH-4626711
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: NOC's 15.7% 30-day decline reflects a fundamental re-rating of program risk. The B-21 Raider is the centerpiece of the USAF's strategic bomber modernization, but Northrop's fixed-price contract leaves the company absorbing cost overruns that have now exceeded $500M cumulatively. Management's inability to guide to a resolution suggests this will be a multi-quarter drag on earnings quality. Investors are questioning whether the defense super-cycle of 2022-2025 was sufficiently priced in competent execution risk on next-generation platforms. The stock trades at ~15x forward P/E — historically inexpensive for a defense prime — but recovers only if B-21 charges stabilize, likely not before Q3 2026.

watchlist_updates.headline: Freeport-McMoRan Q1 2026 beat but 2026 copper/gold guidance cut -300M lbs and -150K oz on Grasberg mine delays; stock -13.1%
watchlist_updates.detail: Freeport-McMoRan Q1 2026: adj. EPS $0.57 (vs. $0.47 est.), revenue $6.23B (+8.8% YoY). However, management cut 2026 copper guidance from 3.4B to 3.1B pounds and gold guidance from 800K to 650K ounces, citing continued Grasberg mine ramp delays following the September 2025 mud-rush incident. Net income $881M (+150% YoY). Stock fell 13.1% on the week. Sources: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/commodities/articles/fcxs-q1-earnings-revenues-top-171200217.html, https://seekingalpha.com/news/4578644-freeport-mcmoran-falls-as-grasberg-delay-cuts-2026-output-outlook

watchlist_updates.headline: Synopsys +11.4% on week — EDA/IP tools remain key enablers of custom AI silicon buildout; multiple analyst target raises
watchlist_updates.detail: Synopsys benefited from the semiconductor AI re-rating. As the leader in EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools, SNPS is a critical upstream enabler for every custom AI chip design at hyperscalers and fabless companies. The pending Ansys acquisition (regulatory clearance expected mid-2026) would expand SNPS into simulation and multiphysics. No earnings this week; next report expected in mid-May.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Synopsys's 20.5% 30-day gain reflects its position as an irreplaceable tool in the custom AI silicon design flow. Every major hyperscaler ASIC program — at Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and Apple — runs through Synopsys Fusion Compiler and PrimeSim signoff tools. The pending Ansys acquisition (regulatory approval expected mid-2026) would extend SNPS into chip-package-board co-simulation, creating a fully integrated hardware design platform. Durability is high given 2-3 year tool contract lock-ins and growing chip design complexity.

watchlist_updates.headline: DigitalOcean +11.2% on week — agentic AI inference cloud narrative; prior $800M equity raise for capacity; Q1 reports May 5
watchlist_updates.detail: DigitalOcean rallied 11.2% after announcing it repositioned as 'the agentic inference cloud' ahead of Q1 2026 results (May 5). In late March, the company raised $800M in a secondary equity offering for AI infrastructure capacity expansion. Investors are looking past near-term dilution to the potential for DOCN to become a mid-market AI inference provider between hyperscalers and bare-metal. Source: https://investors.digitalocean.com/news/news-details/2026/DigitalOcean-Announces-Date-of-First-Quarter-2026-Earnings-Conference-Call/
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: DigitalOcean's 12.1% 30-day gain reflects its repositioning from commodity cloud VMs to 'agentic inference cloud' — a higher-margin segment targeting AI startups and mid-market enterprises that cannot negotiate hyperscaler volume contracts. The $800M equity raise in March provides runway to build GPU inference capacity. Durability depends on whether DOCN can convert this capital into recurring AI workload revenue before AWS/GCP/Azure close the SMB pricing gap.

watchlist_updates.headline: Wix +10.9% on week — AI-powered website builder; no earnings this week; analyst upgrades on AI product differentiation
watchlist_updates.detail: Wix advanced 10.9% on the week on no specific news. The company's AI website builder tools have been cited by analysts as a differentiated offering that could accelerate SMB customer conversion. The stock has a negative 30-day return (-14.1%), suggesting this week's move is a partial catch-up from prior weakness.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Wix's -14.1% 30-day decline (despite this week's +10.9% bounce) reflects ongoing monetization pressure: the shift from DIY to AI-generated websites is compressing traditional subscription ARPU while new AI product lines have not yet scaled. The stock is recovering from concerns about competitive displacement by Squarespace and headless CMS platforms. The bounce may be short-lived unless Q1 2026 results (due May) show AI product subscription growth accelerating.

watchlist_updates.headline: Thermo Fisher Q1 2026 beat but disappointed on organic growth: adj. EPS $5.44 vs. $5.25 est., organic growth only 1%
watchlist_updates.detail: Thermo Fisher Q1 2026: adj. EPS $5.44 (vs. $5.25 est.), revenue $11.01B (+3.5% YoY). Non-GAAP core organic growth of just 1% disappointed analysts expecting 2-3%. Life sciences tool demand remains subdued as biotech/pharma customers continue destocking. Stock dropped 10.8% on the week, erasing a partial recovery. Source: https://www.fiercebiotech.com/medtech/thermo-fisher-sees-higher-q1-earnings-amid-strong-biopharma-demand

watchlist_updates.headline: Keurig Dr Pepper Q1 2026: JDE Peet's acquisition finalized April 1; reaffirmed FY low double-digit EPS growth — stock +10.1%
watchlist_updates.detail: Keurig Dr Pepper Q1 2026 results: reaffirmed full-year guidance for low double-digit EPS growth. The JDE Peet's acquisition (global coffee) closed April 1, 2026, creating a significantly larger global beverages platform. Domestic carbonated soft drink pricing power remains intact. Stock advanced 10.1% on the week. Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/keurig-dr-pepper-inc-q1-165017122.html
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Keurig Dr Pepper's 11.5% 30-day gain combines the JDE Peet's acquisition close (April 1, creating a global coffee platform) with defensive characteristics valued in an uncertain macro environment. The company's diversified beverage portfolio (Dr Pepper, Canada Dry, Snapple, Green Mountain) provides resilient consumer staples cash flows. Durability is supported by consistent pricing power and volume growth; the JDE acquisition adds international coffee exposure that diversifies away from U.S.-centric risk.

watchlist_updates.headline: Teradyne +9.9% on week, +30.6% in 30 days — semiconductor test equipment demand strengthens with AI chip complexity
watchlist_updates.detail: Teradyne's rally continues as complex AI silicon — particularly HBM stacks, advanced packaging, and chiplets — requires significantly more test time per die than standard logic. AI accelerators (HBM, CoWoS packages) are the fastest-growing segment of Teradyne's semiconductor test business. No earnings this week; prior Q1 2026 guidance indicated 15%+ growth in AI-driven semiconductor test.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: TER's 30.6% 30-day gain reflects a structural demand inflection in semiconductor test, where AI chip complexity is extending test time per unit by 2-3x vs. standard logic. The company's EAGLE platform for HBM testing and its memory test systems are at capacity, with lead times extending to 12+ weeks. Durability is supported by multi-year AI infrastructure investment programs at TSMC's CoWoS packaging line and NVIDIA's supply chain ramp. The primary risk is a technology transition to cheaper in-line test architectures that could pressure Teradyne's average selling prices.

watchlist_updates.headline: Accenture hits new 52-week low at $175.45 — IT consulting spend under pressure from corporate AI build-vs-buy decisions
watchlist_updates.detail: Accenture declined 9.8% on the week to a new 52-week low. The most recent earnings (reported March 20) were solid (EPS $2.93 vs. $2.84 est., revenue $18.04B), but bookings growth has slowed. The market is increasingly concerned that enterprise AI adoption is bifurcating: large companies are building internal AI capabilities rather than outsourcing, reducing Accenture's addressable market for traditional IT consulting. Source: https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/accenture-nyseacn-reaches-new-1-year-low-heres-what-happened-2026-04-23/

watchlist_updates.headline: Nucor +9.4% on week — Q1 EPS guidance of $2.70-$2.80 (vs. $0.77 a year ago) confirmed; steel mill backlog up 40% YoY
watchlist_updates.detail: Nucor reports official Q1 2026 results on April 27 after market close; the stock moved this week on positive pre-announcement signals and sector momentum. Prior guidance: EPS $2.70-$2.80 vs. $0.77 in Q1 2025, driven by steel mill backlog up 40% YoY and stronger structural steel prices. A $2.5B 2026 capex plan (down from $3.4B in 2025) signals the company is transitioning from investment to cash generation. Source: https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2887165/nucor-sees-stronger-q1-earnings-on-steel-mills-unit-gains
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: NUE's 31.7% 30-day move reflects a steel cycle recovery overlaid with structural demand from AI data center construction (structural steel) and domestic manufacturing reshoring. The company's mini-mill model with scrap-based production gives it a cost advantage over integrated mills as iron ore prices fluctuate. Four newly completed capital projects are expected to contribute ~$500M in incremental EBITDA above 2025 levels. Tariff tailwinds (25% on imported steel) provide additional pricing power; durability of the rally depends on whether Q1 official results meet the $2.70-2.80 EPS guidance on April 27.

watchlist_updates.headline: Asana +9.4% on week — work management platform rebounds; AI workflow features driving new enterprise land-and-expand motions
watchlist_updates.detail: Asana rebounded 9.4% on the week. The company has been aggressively positioning its AI Studio product as a workflow automation layer that competes with ServiceNow at lower price points for mid-market enterprises. No earnings this week; ASAN reports next in late May 2026.

watchlist_updates.headline: UnitedHealth Q1 2026 beat: adj. EPS $7.23 vs. $6.57 est.; raised FY2026 adj. EPS guide to >$18.25
watchlist_updates.detail: UnitedHealth Group Q1 2026: revenue $111.7B, adj. EPS $7.23 (vs. $6.57 consensus). Raised FY2026 adj. EPS guidance to >$18.25. Optum Health revenue grew 12% YoY; medical cost ratio was 84.3%, roughly in line. Morgan Stanley raised its price target following the print. Stock gained 9.3% on the week, arresting a multi-week slide. Source: https://www.thestreet.com/investing/stocks/morgan-stanley-adjusts-unh-stock-price-target-after-earnings
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: UNH's 30.4% 30-day move encompasses a full earnings cycle recovery. The key driver is Optum Health — the value-based care business that generates higher-margin per-member revenue than traditional insurance. At 25M+ value-based care patients, Optum is on track for 15-20% long-term EBITDA growth. The managed-care sell-off from January through March was driven by Medicare Advantage rate pressure and elevated post-COVID utilization; the Q1 beat suggests the utilization spike has moderated faster than feared. Durability is high if medical cost trends stabilize near the 84% ratio; re-acceleration of hospital utilization remains the key bear-case trigger.

watchlist_updates.headline: Micron +9.2% on week, +25.6% in 30 days — HBM demand narrative strengthened by AI silicon rally
watchlist_updates.detail: No earnings this week; MU rallied on the broad semiconductor AI re-rating and strengthening HBM3E demand from NVIDIA GB200/H200 and AMD MI300X supply chains. Micron is the primary U.S. HBM supplier and benefits disproportionately from any AI cluster expansion. Next earnings: Q3 FY2026 expected late June 2026.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: MU's 25.6% 30-day move reflects a memory cycle recovery overlaid with structural HBM demand from AI accelerators. HBM3E pricing is approximately 5x that of standard DDR5 per die, creating a mix-shift tailwind that could expand Micron's DRAM gross margins from ~35% toward 45%+ by Q4 FY2026. Durability of the move is supported by multi-year supply agreements with NVIDIA and AMD, though DRAM spot prices for legacy applications (PC/mobile) remain soft, representing a partial offset.

watchlist_updates.headline: Danaher Q1 2026 beat but revenue missed: adj. EPS $2.06 vs. $1.96 est., revenue $5.95B vs. $6.06B est.; core organic +0.5%
watchlist_updates.detail: Danaher Q1 2026: adj. EPS $2.06 (+9.5% YoY, vs. $1.96 est.), revenue $5.95B vs. $6.06B consensus. Non-GAAP core revenue growth of just 0.5% reflects continued sluggishness in Diagnostics (-4% core) offsetting Biotechnology strength (+7% core). Raised FY2026 adj. EPS guidance to $8.35-$8.55. Pending acquisition of Masimo Corporation adds complexity to H2 2026 integration expectations. Stock declined 9.0% on the week. Source: https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/danaher-reports-first-quarter-2026-results-302747922.html

watchlist_updates.headline: Agnico Eagle -9.0% on week — gold miner sold off on volume concerns despite strong spot gold prices
watchlist_updates.detail: Agnico Eagle declined 9.0% on the week despite gold prices near all-time highs. The sell-off appears tied to Q1 production results that showed below-consensus gold output at its Canadian operations, partially offset by strong grades at Fosterville (Australia). At $200/share, AEM trades at approximately 1.7x NAV — a premium to peers that leaves limited margin for operational misses.

watchlist_updates.headline: Shift4 Payments -8.8% on week, -13.7% in 30 days — payment processing stock under pressure from volume growth concerns
watchlist_updates.detail: Shift4 Payments declined 8.8% on the week without specific news. The payment processing company (focused on hospitality, restaurants, sports/entertainment) faces volume growth concerns tied to travel/leisure spending normalization. No earnings this week; next results expected in mid-May.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Shift4's -13.7% 30-day decline reflects investor concern that the company's hospitality/restaurant-focused payments volumes could soften if the Iran conflict further impresses on travel demand and consumer discretionary spending. The stock had been a strong performer on sports/entertainment venue expansion, but macro headwinds have removed the premium multiple. Near-term catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings (mid-May) — a volume beat or raised guidance could quickly reverse the decline.

watchlist_updates.headline: TSMC +8.6% on week, +17.2% in 30 days — AI chip demand drives record revenue; N2/N3 capacity fully committed
watchlist_updates.detail: TSMC reported Q1 2026 revenue of NT$839.25B ($26.1B USD), up 42% YoY, driven by AI and HPC chips on N3/N4. Management reiterated that advanced packaging (CoWoS) capacity is fully booked through 2027. The stock benefited from the broader semiconductor AI rally and rising expectations for N2 production ramp in H2 2026. Source: TSMC Q1 2026 Earnings Call, April 17, 2026
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: TSM's 17.2% 30-day gain reflects its position as the irreplaceable bottleneck in advanced AI silicon manufacturing. N3 utilization remains above 95%, driven by NVIDIA Blackwell, Apple A-series, and AMD MI350 tape-outs. The N2 ramp in H2 2026 and Arizona fab buildout (with U.S. CHIPS Act subsidies) represent additional revenue vectors. Durability is high — TSMC has no credible near-term competitor in leading-edge logic below 3nm — though geopolitical Taiwan risk remains the perennial overhang.

watchlist_updates.headline: United Airlines Q1 2026 record revenue $14.6B (+10.6% YoY) but full-year EPS guidance slashed to $7-$11 (from $12-$14) on jet-fuel surge
watchlist_updates.detail: United Airlines Q1 2026: revenue $14.6B (record, +10.6% YoY), adj. EPS $1.19 (within prior $1.00-$1.50 guidance). Full-year EPS guidance cut to $7-$11 (from $12-$14) as Iran conflict pushes jet fuel toward $4.30/gallon in Q2. United is removing ~5 points of H2 capacity including Tel Aviv and Dubai routes. Q2 EPS guide of $1.00-$2.00 missed analyst consensus of $2.08. Stock declined 8.6% on the week. Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/united-airlines-ual-q1-2026-earnings.html

watchlist_updates.headline: IBM Q1 2026 beat but flat full-year guidance: EPS $1.91 vs. $1.81 est., revenue $15.92B vs. $15.77B est. — stock -8.5%
watchlist_updates.detail: IBM Q1 2026: adj. EPS $1.91 (vs. $1.81 est.), revenue $15.92B (vs. $15.77B est.). Software segment grew 7% YoY, driven by Red Hat and AI Consulting bookings. However, management maintained full-year guidance rather than raising it, signaling slower-than-expected AI software revenue ramp. The stock declined 8.5% on the week. Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/ibm-q1-earnings-report-2026.html

watchlist_updates.headline: Western Digital +8.4% on week, +34.2% in 30 days — benefiting from NAND pricing recovery and AI storage demand
watchlist_updates.detail: Western Digital continues to rally alongside SNDK on the NAND pricing recovery narrative and AI data storage buildout. WDC's HDD business (kept in the parent company after the SNDK spin-off) is also benefiting from nearline demand similar to STX. Post-spin-off capital structure is cleaner, enabling more aggressive buybacks and debt reduction.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Western Digital's 34.2% 30-day gain reflects the NAND pricing recovery cycle combined with AI data storage demand. The spin-off of SanDisk (flash) leaves WDC with the HDD business (nearline, enterprise, and consumer) that benefits from AI training data lake buildout. Management has guided to >$35B in TAM by 2027 across its HDD portfolio as data generation from AI inference accelerates. Durability depends on HDD pricing discipline and the NAND cycle not rolling over before WDC completes its balance sheet repair.

watchlist_updates.headline: Devon Energy +8.4% on week — elevated WTI crude (~$95) supports E&P cash flows; no earnings this week
watchlist_updates.detail: Devon rallied alongside peer E&P names (FANG +8.1%, OXY +6.2%) as WTI crude held above $94/bbl. At $95 WTI, Devon's breakeven model supports a variable + fixed dividend yielding approximately 8-10% annualized and $2-3B in annual buybacks. No earnings this week; Q1 2026 results expected early May.

watchlist_updates.headline: Arista Networks +7.7% on week, +35.2% in 30 days — 400/800G AI data center Ethernet switching capturing share from InfiniBand
watchlist_updates.detail: Arista Networks rallied 7.7% on the week. The company is the primary beneficiary of the industry shift from InfiniBand (NVIDIA NVLink clusters) toward Ethernet-based AI cluster networking for scale-out deployments. Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all major ANET customers for their 800G AI fabric buildouts. No earnings this week; Q1 2026 results expected late April/early May.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: ANET's 35.2% 30-day move reflects a structural customer-mix shift: hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending is migrating from small-cluster (InfiniBand) to large-cluster (Ethernet) architectures where Arista's platforms excel. At 800G and eventually 1.6T port speeds, Arista has a 12-18 month technology lead over Cisco's data center switching platform. FY2025 revenue of $7B+ and 40%+ operating margins make this one of the few high-growth large-caps with immediate earnings power. Durability is high unless InfiniBand regains ground in the next GPU architecture transition (Blackwell Ultra/Rubin).

watchlist_updates.headline: SanDisk +7.5% on week, +40.9% in 30 days — flash storage demand recovery driven by AI data lake build-out
watchlist_updates.detail: SanDisk (formerly WD's flash business, spun out 2025) continues to rally on NAND pricing recovery and AI data storage demand. Enterprise SSD pricing is recovering as AI training data lakes and inference serving infrastructure require petabyte-scale flash storage. No earnings this week. The 40.9% 30-day move reflects both the NAND cycle recovery and AI storage demand.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: SanDisk's 40.9% 30-day gain (post-spin from WDC in 2025) reflects both a NAND pricing recovery and AI data storage structural demand. Enterprise SSDs for AI training (requiring high-endurance, high-throughput NVMe) represent a higher-margin product mix than consumer flash. The company is pursuing capacity rationalization — reducing NAND bit output growth to support pricing — which, if sustained across the industry (Samsung, SK Hynix), could drive further ASP recovery through 2026.

watchlist_updates.headline: Capital One Q1 2026 revenue miss ($15.2B vs. $15.4B est.); adj. EPS $4.42 missed estimates; charge-off rate 3.45%; stock -7.3%
watchlist_updates.detail: Capital One Q1 2026: revenue $15.2B (-2% QoQ, vs. $15.4B est.), adj. EPS $4.42 (missed consensus). Net charge-offs $3.8B; charge-off rate 3.45%; provision $4.1B. CET1 14.4%; deposits +3% to $489.1B. Discover integration described as 'going well'; full system migration expected by Q1 2027. Morgan Stanley cut target to $273 (from $300); Bank of America cut to $236 (from $254). Sources: https://www.tikr.com/blog/capital-one-stock-drops-after-q1-2026-earnings-miss-is-cof-a-buy-now, https://stocktitan.net/sec-filings/COF/

watchlist_updates.headline: CF Industries +7.3% on week — nitrogen fertilizer pricing recovers on geopolitical supply disruption concerns
watchlist_updates.detail: CF Industries advanced 7.3% on the week. Nitrogen fertilizer prices have recovered as Black Sea/Ukraine supply disruptions limit global urea and ammonia availability. Iran geopolitical risk adds a natural gas supply uncertainty that supports nitrogen pricing. No earnings this week; Q1 2026 results expected early May.

watchlist_updates.headline: Manhattan Associates +7.3% on week — supply chain management software continues to see strong demand from retail/logistics AI transformation
watchlist_updates.detail: Manhattan Associates advanced 7.3% on the week. The company's cloud WMS (Warehouse Management System) and TMS (Transportation Management System) platforms are gaining share as retailers invest in AI-driven logistics automation. No earnings this week.

watchlist_updates.headline: Seagate +7.0% on week, +38.0% in 30 days — nearline HDD demand for AI data centers driving pricing power recovery
watchlist_updates.detail: Seagate's rally reflects a structural shift: AI training data sets and cold/warm storage in hyperscaler data centers are driving nearline HDD demand at volumes that support a multi-year pricing recovery. STX management guided to 50TB+ drives in production in 2026, which carries significantly higher ASPs. No earnings this week.
watchlist_updates.deep_dive: Seagate's 38.0% 30-day gain is driven by a nearline HDD demand inflection. Hyperscalers are deploying HDD-based cold/warm storage at massive scale for AI training datasets (which are too large to store entirely on flash). STX's HAMR (heat-assisted magnetic recording) technology enables 30-50TB drives in production now, with 50TB+ on the roadmap. At 50TB+ per drive, HAMR drives carry 30-40% higher ASPs than conventional drives, expanding margins dramatically. Durability is high for 2-3 years given the physical constraints on alternatives.

watchlist_updates.headline: Corning +7.0% on week, +23.9% in 30 days — optical fiber demand for AI data center interconnect surging
